<!-- Generated by agents-reverse-engineer v1.2.8 -->

# src/generation

Orchestrates AI-powered documentation generation by transforming file/directory discovery plans into dependency-ordered execution tasks that produce .sum file summaries and AGENTS.md aggregations via Claude prompts enforcing density, behavioral contract preservation, and incremental update semantics.

## Contents

**[collector.ts](./collector.ts)** — `collectAgentsDocs(projectRoot)` / `collectAnnexFiles(projectRoot)` recursively walk trees returning `{ relativePath, content }` tuples for AGENTS.md and .annex.sum files, skip `SKIP_DIRS` (node_modules, .git, dist, build, vendor, etc.), silently ignore permission-denied paths.

**[complexity.ts](./complexity.ts)** — `analyzeComplexity(files, projectRoot)` computes `ComplexityMetrics` (fileCount, directoryDepth via path separator counting, directories Set via upward parent traversal) from file path array.

**[executor.ts](./executor.ts)** — `buildExecutionPlan(plan, projectRoot, variant?)` converts `GenerationPlan` to `ExecutionPlan` with `ExecutionTask` array (file tasks with zero dependencies sorted depth-descending, directory tasks with dependencies on child file task IDs sorted post-order deepest-first), `isDirectoryComplete(dir, expectedFiles, root, variant?)` checks .sum existence via `sumFileExists()`, `getReadyDirectories(plan, variant?)` returns dirs where all child .sum files exist, `formatExecutionPlanAsMarkdown(plan)` renders GENERATION-PLAN.md with Phase 1 (files grouped by dir), Phase 2 (dirs by depth), checkbox `- [ ]` format.

**[types.ts](./types.ts)** — `SummaryMetadata` interface defines `purpose: string`, optional `criticalTodos: string[]` (security/breaking issues only), `relatedFiles: string[]` (tightly coupled siblings).

## Subdirectories

**[prompts/](./prompts/)** — Prompt template library defining system/user prompts for .sum file analysis and AGENTS.md generation, enforcing density rules (≥1 identifier per sentence, zero filler phrases), behavioral contract preservation (verbatim regex/constants/workflow rules), annex overflow patterns, incremental update preservation, path accuracy mandates; `builder.ts` orchestrates template assembly with file content, import maps, manifests, existing docs; `templates.ts` defines five prompt constants (`FILE_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `FILE_UPDATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `DIRECTORY_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `DIRECTORY_UPDATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT`); `types.ts` exports `PromptContext` and `SUMMARY_GUIDELINES` (300-500 char target).

**[writers/](./writers/)** — Serializes .sum (YAML frontmatter + summary text), .annex.sum (markdown fence for oversize constants), AGENTS.md (generated marker comment + optional user .local.md import), CLAUDE.md (pointer importing AGENTS.md); `sum.ts` handles frontmatter parsing via regex (`/^---\n([\s\S]*?)\n---\n/`), array formatting (inline `[a,b,c]` if ≤3 items <40 chars else multi-line `  - item`), variant paths (`.${variant}.sum`); `agents-md.ts` / `claude-md.ts` detect user-authored files via `GENERATED_MARKER_PREFIX` absence, preserve as .local.md with `@{filename}.local.md` import.

## Architecture

Pipeline stages: (1) `collectAgentsDocs` / `collectAnnexFiles` gather existing artifacts, (2) `buildExecutionPlan` transforms orchestrator `GenerationPlan` into post-order execution graph (file tasks at deepest paths first, directory tasks depend on child file task completion), (3) prompt assembly via `buildFilePrompt` / `buildDirectoryPrompt` injects source/context/imports/manifests into templates, (4) writers serialize LLM responses as .sum (frontmatter + summary) or AGENTS.md (marker comment + aggregated content), (5) `isDirectoryComplete` / `getReadyDirectories` track .sum existence for incremental directory processing.

## Behavioral Contracts

**Skip list (collector.ts):** `['node_modules', '.git', '.agents-reverse-engineer', 'vendor', 'dist', 'build', '__pycache__', '.next', 'venv', '.venv', 'target', '.cargo', '.gradle']`

**Task ID patterns (executor.ts):** `file:${relativePath}` for file tasks, `dir:${relativePath}` for directory tasks

**Traversal ordering (executor.ts):** File tasks sorted by `getDirectoryDepth()` descending (deepest first), directory tasks sorted post-order (deepest first) ensuring child AGENTS.md exist before parent aggregation

**YAML frontmatter regex (writers/sum.ts):** `/^---\n([\s\S]*?)\n---\n/` for delimiter, `/generated_at:\s*(.+)/` / `/content_hash:\s*(.+)/` / `/purpose:\s*(.+)/` for field extraction, `/key:\s*\[([^\]]*)\]/` for inline arrays, `/key:\s*\n((?:\s+-\s+.+\n?)+)/m` for multi-line arrays

**Generation marker (writers/agents-md.ts):** `GENERATED_MARKER_PREFIX = '<!-- Generated by agents-reverse-engineer'` (version-agnostic prefix for detection), `GENERATED_MARKER` appends version via `getVersion()`

**Array formatting (writers/sum.ts):** Inline `[a, b, c]` if ≤3 items and max length <40 chars, else multi-line with `  - ` prefix

**Manifest filenames (prompts/builder.ts):** `['package.json', 'Cargo.toml', 'go.mod', 'pyproject.toml', 'pom.xml', 'build.gradle', 'Gemfile', 'composer.json', 'CMakeLists.txt', 'Makefile']`

**Source file pattern (prompts/builder.ts):** `/\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx|py|go|rs|java|kt)$/` for import extraction

**Compression threshold (prompts/builder.ts):** `compressionRatio < 0.5` triggers dynamic size targets (`sourceSize * ratio`, max `target * 1.2`) and ultra-compression techniques

**Language map (prompts/builder.ts):** `.ts`, `.tsx`, `.js`, `.jsx`, `.py`, `.rb`, `.go`, `.rs`, `.java`, `.kt`, `.swift`, `.cs`, `.php`, `.vue`, `.svelte`, `.json`, `.yaml`, `.yml`, `.md`, `.css`, `.scss`, `.html` → syntax highlighting language, default `text`

## Workflow & Conventions

**Density enforcement:** Every sentence must reference ≥1 identifier (function/class/type/const name), prohibited filler phrases ("this file", "provides", "responsible for", "is used to"), use telegraphic shorthand ("exports X, Y, Z" not "this module exports...").

**Anchor term preservation:** All exported symbols must appear exactly as written in source (preserve casing), missing any export constitutes failure.

**Behavioral contracts never excluded:** Regex patterns (verbatim in backticks), format strings, magic constants, prompt templates, error codes, env var names, file format specs, workflow rules (branch naming, commit formats, testing mandates, CLI commands, linter configs, approval workflows, AI agent instructions, code conventions).

**Incremental update preservation:** When `existingSum` / `existingAgentsMd` present, preserve unchanged sections verbatim (don't rephrase/reorganize), update only code-affected content, maintain behavioral contracts and annex references unless source modified them.

**Path accuracy (AGENTS.md generation):** Reference files/modules using only paths from Import Map section, never invent/rename/guess module paths, use exact directory names from Project Directory Structure, omit cross-reference if path uncertain.

**User content preservation:** User-authored AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md detected by absence of `GENERATED_MARKER_PREFIX`, renamed to .local.md on first generation, preserved via `@{filename}.local.md` import directive ensuring AI agents see user content before generated content.

**Variant routing:** `variant` parameter writes `.${variant}.sum` and `AGENTS.${variant}.md`, hub files import `@AGENTS.${activeVariant}.md`, variant files bypass user-preservation logic.

**Output format:** Start response directly with bold purpose statement, no preamble/meta-commentary, AGENTS.md first line must be exactly `<!-- Generated by agents-reverse-engineer -->`.
